title: Parametrized view with enabled analyzer often near to 2x slower then with old analyzer
description: ### Company or project name _No response_ ### Describe the situation Initially problem described here https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/issues/87267 and now old analyzer not work We use cascade of param views (one param views invoke another multiple param views). With old analyzer queries executed near 2x time faster then with new ### Which ClickHouse versions are affected? ch v25.8.2.29 ### How to reproduce something like be this pattern drop database if exists my_db on cluster analytics; create database my_db on cluster analytics; CREATE TABLE my_db.my_table on cluster analytics ENGINE = ReplicatedMergeTree ORDER BY id AS SELECT number as id FROM numbers(3); CREATE VIEW my_db.my_view1 on cluster analytics AS WITH {param_id:Int64} as param_id SELECT * FROM my_db.my_table where id=param_id; CREATE VIEW my_db.my_view2 on cluster analytics AS with cte as ( SELECT * FROM my_db.my_view1(param_id={param_id:Int64}) ) select * from cte; but we have big queries and logic with multiple tables ### Expected performance like with old analyzer (not 2x slower) ### Additional context _No response_
poc: drop database if exists my_db on cluster analytics;
create database my_db on cluster analytics;
CREATE TABLE my_db.my_table on cluster analytics ENGINE = ReplicatedMergeTree ORDER BY id AS SELECT number as id FROM numbers(3);

CREATE VIEW my_db.my_view1  on cluster analytics AS
WITH
  {param_id:Int64}         as param_id
SELECT * FROM my_db.my_table
where id=param_id;
key_bug_features: Parametrized views with new analyzer are significantly slower
bug_features_seed: DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS my_db ON CLUSTER analytics;
CREATE DATABASE my_db ON CLUSTER analytics;
CREATE TABLE my_db.my_table (id Int64, name String, created_at DateTime DEFAULT now()) ENGINE = ReplicatedMergeTree() ORDER BY id;
CREATE VIEW my_db.my_view1 AS SELECT * FROM my_db.my_table WHERE id = {param_id:Int64};
CREATE VIEW my_db.my_view2 AS SELECT * FROM my_db.my_view1(param_id = CAST(NULL AS Int64));
